mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and strong consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates significantly, it erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, prompting central bank tightening, which collectively leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4.0% for 3 consecutive months, yet S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with a low unemployment rate signals strong consumer demand and a tight labor market, indicating a healthy economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If both UNRATE is below 5.0% and RSAFS 3-month average growth exceeds 0.5%, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 4-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated within a monetary tightening cycle, it increases the cost of capital for businesses and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains above 0.75% for 2 consecutive months during a tightening cycle, yet S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals growing consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and demand for related goods and services, which is positive for corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average increases by > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:housing market is a leading indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month moving average increases by > 5% month-over-month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing output and capacity utilization, indicating healthy supply-side conditions that support corporate profitability and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and potentially higher interest rates by the central bank, ultimately compressing corporate margins and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.0% for 6 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:tight labor market can lead to inflation
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 4.0% for 6 consecutive months, yet S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with moderate inflation signals a healthy and stable economic expansion, fostering an environment of rising corporate profits and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL annualized growth < 3.0%
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.0% and CPIAUCSL annualized growth is below 3.0%, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.
